summary: Legacy validated record without machine-checkable validation proof. Policy-backed control for stopping Windows Update from being used as a device-driver source and for excluding driver-classified updates from Windows quality updates. This can reduce unexpected driver changes, but it can also block useful driver delivery when no local or vendor-provided driver is available.

setting.name: Windows Update Driver Search Policy
setting.casual_explanation: When Windows needs a device driver, it can search Windows Update for one. These policies control whether that search is allowed and, if it is, when it happens.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it when they want tighter control over driver versions or want to avoid surprise driver replacements from Windows Update. They leave it enabled when they want Windows to help find drivers automatically.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off gives more manual control over driver sourcing, but it can make new-device setup or driver recovery less convenient.
setting.professional_notes: DeviceSetup.admx documents SearchOrderConfig under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DriverSearching with enum values 1 for always search Windows Update, 2 for search only if needed, and 0 for never search Windows Update. ICM.admx documents DontSearchWindowsUpdate under the same key with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. WindowsUpdate.admx documents ExcludeWUDriversInQualityUpdate under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The current app writes all three values.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows keeps the normal driver-source search behavior without this explicit policy override.

allowed_values.label: Do not search Windows Update
allowed_values.meaning: If this policy is enabled, Windows does not search Windows Update for device drivers.

allowed_values.label: Always search Windows Update
allowed_values.meaning: If this policy is enabled, Windows always searches Windows Update for device drivers.

allowed_values.label: Search Windows Update only if needed
allowed_values.meaning: If this policy is enabled, Windows searches Windows Update for device drivers only when needed.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows keeps the normal driver-search behavior without this explicit security option override.

allowed_values.label: Windows Update search allowed
allowed_values.meaning: If the policy is disabled, Windows Update can be searched for drivers when no local drivers are present.

allowed_values.label: Windows Update search blocked
allowed_values.meaning: If the policy is enabled, Windows Update is not searched for drivers when no local drivers are present.

allowed_values.label: Windows includes driver-classified updates
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Update can include updates that have a Driver classification.

allowed_values.label: Do not exclude driver-classified quality updates
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and Windows Update can include driver-classified updates.

allowed_values.label: Exclude driver-classified quality updates
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Update does not include driver-classified updates in Windows quality updates.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes SearchOrderConfig = 0, DontSearchWindowsUpdate = 1, and ExcludeWUDriversInQualityUpdate = 1. Together these values align with the documented direction of keeping Windows Update out of the driver's sourcing and quality-update path.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems supporting the DriverSearching policies

states.rationale: Leave the Windows Update driver-search block unset.
Leave the driver-exclusion quality-update policy unset.

windows_defaults.notes: This record only validates the official policy mappings, not vendor-specific driver deployment workflows.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
intended_for: Systems that rely on Windows to help locate drivers
avoid_for: Machines that must avoid Windows Update supplied drivers

states.rationale: Leave the Windows Update block policy unset.
Leave the quality-update driver exclusion policy unset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal driver search behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want tighter manual control over driver sourcing.
tradeoffs: Windows can continue to help locate drivers.
Driver changes from Windows Update remain possible.

recommended_profiles.label: No Windows Update driver search
avoid_for: General users who depend on automatic driver discovery

states.rationale: Set the search order policy to never search Windows Update.
Explicitly block Windows Update driver searching when no local drivers are present.
Exclude driver-classified updates from Windows quality updates.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows Update kept out of your driver sourcing path.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Windows to help automatically find missing or newer drivers.
tradeoffs: Unexpected Windows Update driver replacements are reduced.
Driver installation and recovery can require more manual work.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DeviceSetup.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps SearchOrderConfig under DriverSearching and defines enum values 1 for always search Windows Update, 2 for search only if needed, and 0 for do not search Windows Update.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DeviceSetup.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text explains the three search-order choices and their effect on Windows Update driver searching.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ICM.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps DontSearchWindowsUpdate under DriverSearching with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ICM.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy stops Windows Update driver searching when no local driver is present, while disabling it always searches Windows Update in that scenario.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsUpdate.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps ExcludeWUDriversInQualityUpdate under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WindowsUpdate.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy excludes driver-classified updates from Windows quality updates, while disabling or not configuring it allows those updates to be included.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes SearchOrderConfig = 0, DontSearchWindowsUpdate = 1, and ExcludeWUDriversInQualityUpdate = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: The record uses exact Microsoft policy mappings for all three values, the app writes those same values, and the restore path is clear. The bundle is still high-impact, but that is a risk question rather than a class blocker.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: DeviceSetup.admx: <policy name="DriverSearchPlaces_SearchOrderConfiguration" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DriverSearching"> ... <enum ... valueName="SearchOrderConfig"> ... <decimal value="0"/>; ICM.admx: key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DriverSearching" valueName="DontSearchWindowsUpdate"; WindowsUpdate.admx: key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate" valueName="ExcludeWUDriversInQualityUpdate"> <enabledValue><decimal value="1" /></enabledValue>.
validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft policy files define the exact DriverSearching and WindowsUpdate policy paths used by the app. SearchOrderConfig = 0 means do not search Windows Update, DontSearchWindowsUpdate = 1 blocks Windows Update device-driver searching, and ExcludeWUDriversInQualityUpdate = 1 excludes driver-classified updates from Windows quality updates.
